title: Senior Manager Market Management USA
_note: Duplicate posting of job_id 4411725603 (same role, different listing). Scored independently per analysis rules.
required_skills: University degree in Business Administration, Economics, Law or comparable field
Several years of experience in financial services and primary insurance
Understanding of US business culture, decision-making, and stakeholder management with US-based leadership teams
Excellent English (min. C1) and German (min. C1)
matched_skills: University degree (Master Management & Entrepreneurship + Master PR & Advertising + Bachelor Management Administration)
Financial services experience — HSBC PMO role (digital transformation programme)
Strong financial and analytical skills (USD 8M budget governance, burn-rate dashboards, KPI framework design, ROI analytics)
Strategic mindset and initiative (GTM strategy at IPC for 4 product launches, portfolio gap analysis at GRG, competitive intelligence at ECE)
Stakeholder management — cross-functional, C-suite, 6 workstreams (HSBC; Hapag-Lloyd)
Decision-ready analysis and C-level executive reporting (C-suite escalation, executive investment decisions at HSBC)
Programme governance and oversight (PMO Operations, risk escalation, prioritisation management)
missing_skills: German C1 (required minimum) — CV states B1 only
Primary insurance industry experience — CV covers financial services (banking/fintech) but not insurance; HDI is a major insurer
US business culture and US-based leadership engagement — no US market exposure in CV (EMEA/APAC coverage only)
M&A experience including due diligence and post-merger integration
Dedicated strategy department background — CV experience is PMO/operations-side rather than corporate strategy function
core_responsibilities: Supporting implementation of specific strategic objectives for the USA market
Identifying growth opportunities and supporting market and business planning
Analyzing performance (growth, profitability, costs) and deriving improvement initiatives
Preparing decision-ready analyses and planning/budgeting documents for senior management
Managing strategic projects and ad-hoc board assignments; supporting M&A activities including due diligence and post-merger integration
Fostering collaboration between US entities and global central functions
Assuming selected governance and oversight responsibilities in US entities
recommended_emphasis: Lead with HSBC Programme Management Officer: USD 8M digital transformation budget governance, C-suite reporting, 6-workstream coordination — this is the closest analogue to the strategic planning and governance remit in this role

Highlight decision-ready analysis and executive data storytelling (budget variance dashboards, C-suite escalation, investment decision support) to address the 'entscheidungsreife Analysen' requirement directly
Frame cross-functional coordination across Product, Engineering, Marketing, and C-suite at HSBC as international stakeholder management relevant to US-HQ collaboration
Position ECE competitive intelligence and market analysis work, plus GRG portfolio gap analysis, as evidence of strategic thinking and market planning capability

Proactively acknowledge German language development trajectory (B1 → progressing toward C1); emphasise English C1 and Chinese C2 as multi-market communication strengths; note that the US-focused role demands strong English above all
summary: Moderate match (73.8). The candidate has solid alignment on analytical depth, executive stakeholder management, and programme governance — core competencies for this senior strategic role at HDI. The HSBC PMO experience (USD 8M budget, C-suite reporting, multi-workstream governance) directly maps to the role's budgeting, analysis, and decision-support responsibilities. GTM and market intelligence work at IPC, GRG, and ECE supports the market planning and strategic thinking requirements. Key gaps remain: German language is required at C1 but the candidate is at B1; there is no insurance industry background (HDI is a Tier-1 German insurer); US market experience is absent from the CV; and M&A/due diligence exposure is not evidenced. Despite these gaps, the breadth of financial services governance experience and the strategic project profile make this a borderline strong-moderate application worth submitting with a well-targeted cover letter that reframes the financial services PMO experience as transferable to an insurance strategy context.
